title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Proxy Trust, and Client Identity · Contract Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for request targets, host authority, proxy trust, and client identity and demands the smallest safe request authority and trusted-proxy decision record with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.
Validate canonical host and port against configured authority before routing, link generation, cache keys, redirects, or tenant selection.
Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Express Request Field Meaning · changed case · Quiz Express Request Field M…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 2703 in a document service receiving slow compressed uploads. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive authority only across an explicit trusted-proxy and host allow-list boundary; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
